title: 10 best herbal tinctures for cold sore
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for cold sores are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, prepared by soaking the plant parts in a solvent such as alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures can help alleviate cold sore symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antiviral properties. Examples of herbal tinctures that can help with cold sores include Lysine, Echinacea, St. John's Wort, and Licorice Root. Lysine tincture can help prevent cold sore outbreaks by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus. Echinacea and St. John's Wort have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain. Licorice Root tincture can soothe irritation and promote healing. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can experience faster recovery, reduced discomfort, and improved quality of life during cold sore outbreaks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Belladonna tincture helps with cold sores because it contains alkaloids that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These alkaloids can help reduce the pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores. Additionally, belladonna tincture may help to dry out the cold sore, which can speed up the healing process. The antiviral properties of belladonna tincture can also help to prevent the spread of the virus that causes cold sores. By reducing the severity and duration of cold sores, belladonna tincture can provide relief and promote faster healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Belladonna tincture contains alkaloids such as atropine and scopolamine that help reduce inflammation and swelling, providing relief from the painful symptoms of cold sores.
Analgesic: The alkaloids present in belladonna tincture, particularly hyoscyamine, act as painkillers, helping to alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by cold sores.
Antispasmodic: Belladonna tincture can help relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can help prevent the spread of the cold sore virus and promote healing.
Astringent: Belladonna tincture has a drying effect, which can help to dry out the cold sore and reduce its size and duration.
Immune-boosting: Belladonna tincture contains compounds that can help to boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off the virus that causes cold sores and prevent future outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in alkaloids, the flowers are used in tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, beneficial for cold sore treatment.
Leaves: Containing high levels of scopolamine, atropine, and hyoscyamine, the leaves are used in tinctures to help reduce pain and inflammation associated with cold sores.
Roots: Packed with medicinal alkaloids, the roots are used in tinctures for their antispasmodic and analgesic properties, providing relief for cold sore symptoms.
Berries/Seeds: Although potentially toxic if ingested, the berries or seeds can be used topically in tinctures for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in cold sore healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh belladonna leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day.
Wash the plant material in cold running water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and soak it in 100 proof vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, retaining the liquid extract slowly.
Transfer the clear tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place forever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with cold sores because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb contains flavonoids, carotenoids, and volatile oils, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. The antimicrobial properties may help prevent the virus from spreading and infecting other cells. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can promote wound healing, helping the cold sore to heal faster. Its soothing effect can also provide relief from itching and discomfort caused by cold sores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sores, thanks to the active constituents like flavonoids and triterpenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like lutein and zeaxanthin, help prevent the spread of the herpes simplex virus causing cold sores.
Antiviral: Pot marigold tincture has been shown to have antiviral effects, particularly against the herpes simplex virus, due to the active constituents such as quercetin and rutin.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of pot marigold tincture, resulting from active compounds like salicylic acid, help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by cold sores.
Wound-healing: Its wound-healing properties, owing to compounds like tannins and saponins, promote tissue regeneration and help speed up the healing process of cold sores.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for treating cold sores.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain compounds that can help reduce pain and inflammation, making them useful for cold sore treatment.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help soothe and heal cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Cleanly prepare the harvested flowers by gently drying them with clean paper towels or a salad spinner.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining liquid tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with cold sores because it contains high levels of antioxidants, particularly catechins, which have been shown to have anti-viral properties. These properties can help to reduce the severity and duration of cold sores by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which is responsible for causing cold sores. Additionally, white tea has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. The tincture form of white tea also allows for easy application directly to the affected area, providing quick and direct relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea tincture can reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sores, providing relief and promoting healing. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.

Antiviral: White tea tincture has antiviral properties that can help inhibit the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores, from replicating. The active constituents responsible for this property are catechins and polyphenols.

Antioxidant: White tea tincture contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and supporting the healing process. The primary antioxidants in white tea are flavonoids and polyphenols.

Analgesic: White tea tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with cold sores. This is due to the presence of various compounds, including flavonoids and tannins.

Immune-boosting: White tea tincture can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off infections and promoting faster healing. This property is attributed to the high concentration of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, white tea leaves help reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
Buds: High in tannins and catechins, white tea buds can help dry out cold sores and promote healing.
Flowers: Containing antiviral and antibacterial compounds, white tea flowers can help prevent the spread of the cold sore virus.
Stems: Packed with antioxidants, white tea stems aid in reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair for cold sores.
Bark: With natural astringent properties, white tea bark can help dry out cold sores and speed up the healing process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality camellia sinensis leaves at peak freshness for optimal flavor and potency.
Dry the fresh leaves using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part white tea powder with 2 parts vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar for 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and bottle the clear tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with cold sore because it has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores. The active compounds in gotu kola, including asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. Additionally, gotu kola may help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of cold sores and speed up the healing process. By boosting the immune system and improving circulation, gotu kola tincture can also help to prevent future outbreaks of cold sores.
remedy_properties: Wound healing promotion: Gotu kola tincture can help cold sores heal faster due to its active constituents, including asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid, which stimulate collagen synthesis and tissue regeneration.
Anti-inflammatory effect: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the triterpenoid saponins, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.

Antiviral activity: Gotu kola tincture has been shown to inhibit herpes simplex virus (HSV) replication, thanks to its active compounds like brahmoside and brahminoside, which can prevent the virus from causing further outbreaks.

Immune system support: The tincture can enhance the immune response, allowing the body to better fight off the HSV infection, as it contains several immune-boosting compounds, such as flavonoids and essential oils.

Pain relief: Gotu kola tincture provides pain relief for cold sores, as it contains compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which have analgesic properties that can alleviate discomfort caused by cold sores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Gotu kola leaves are the most commonly used part of the plant in medicinal tinctures for cold sores due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties.

Rhyzomes: Gotu kola rhyzomes are also used in tinctures for cold sores because they contain high levels of asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid, which have been shown to have antiviral and immune-boosting effects.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or rhyzomes, gotu kola stems can still be used in tinctures for cold sores due to their triterpenoid content, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Roots: Although less commonly used than the above parts, gotu kola roots can still be used in tinctures for cold sores due to their content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality gotu kola leaves and flowers fresh or dried for optimal potency.
Cleanse and dry the herbs thoroughly with a soft brush removing any dirt debris.
Combine one part of gotu kola herb material with two parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-30 days to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with cold sores because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient known for its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties make it effective in reducing the redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the virus that causes cold sores. The tincture form of eastern purple coneflower allows for quick absorption into the bloodstream, providing fast-acting relief. By using this tincture at the first sign of a cold sore, it can help to shorten the duration and severity of the outbreak.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can reduce inflammation and swelling, providing relief from the painful symptoms of cold sores. This property is due to the presence of chicoric and caftaric acids.

Antiviral: The tincture can inhibit the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores, from replicating. This is attributed to the active constituent, echinacea, which has been shown to have antiviral properties.

Immune-boosting: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. This is due to the presence of alkylamides, which have been shown to stimulate the immune system.

Pain-relieving: The tincture can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with cold sores. This property is due to the presence of essential oils, such as humulene and caryophyllene, which have analgesic properties.

Wound-healing: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help speed up the healing process of cold sores. This is due to the presence of polysaccharides, which have been shown to promote tissue regeneration and wound healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-viral properties, the flowers of the eastern purple coneflower are used to extract oils that can help reduce cold sore outbreaks.
Leaves: High in anti-inflammatory and anti-viral compounds, the leaves of the eastern purple coneflower are used to create potent tinctures for cold sore treatment.
Buds: Containing high levels of antioxidants, the buds of the eastern purple coneflower are used to make medicinal tinctures that can help soothe and heal cold sores.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, the seeds of the eastern purple coneflower are used to create tinctures that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.
Rhizomes: Rich in anti-viral and anti-inflammatory compounds, the rhizomes of the eastern purple coneflower are used to make medicinal tinctures that can help prevent and treat cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and foliage in late summer for optimal potency.
Combine 100-200 grams of plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Bottle the resulting tincture in amber glass containers and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with cold sore because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which is responsible for causing cold sores. Additionally, licorice tincture can help soothe the pain and inflammation associated with cold sores, providing relief to those suffering from this condition. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative to over-the-counter treatments, which may contain harsh chemicals with potential side effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture can reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sores, providing relief from pain and discomfort. This property is due to the triterpenoid glycyrrhizin.

Antiviral: Licorice tincture has the ability to inhibit the herpes simplex virus, preventing the spread and outbreak of cold sores. The flavonoids and isoflavonoids in licorice are responsible for this antiviral effect.

Demulcent: Licorice tincture can form a protective film over the cold sore, providing a soothing effect and promoting healing. This is attributed to the polysaccharides and glycoproteins found in licorice.

Immune-boosting: Licorice tincture can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off the herpes simplex virus. The active constituents responsible for this include glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and isoflavonoids.
Analgesic: Licorice tincture can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cold sores. The compound glycyrrhetinic acid is responsible for this analgesic effect.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating cold sores.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain isoflavonoids, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating cold sores.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain coumarins, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating cold sores.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots and wash them gently with cool water to remove dirt debris.
Slice the cleaned roots into thin pieces and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried root material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to allow infusion to occur slowly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to amber bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with cold sores because it contains properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation, common symptoms of cold sores. The tannins found in witch hazel have astringent properties, which can help dry out the blisters and promote healing. Additionally, witch hazel has antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help prevent the spread of the virus and protect the cold sore from infection. Its soothing and calming effects can also provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with cold sores. Applying witch hazel tincture to a cold sore can help speed up the healing process and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cold sores, due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help dry out the cold sore and speed up healing by causing constriction of bodily tissues and protein precipitation, owed to the high tannin content.
Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture's antioxidant properties protect the skin from damage and support the healing process, thanks to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and essential oils.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of witch hazel tincture help prevent cold sores from becoming infected and reduce the risk of scarring, attributed mainly to the tannins and volatile oils.
Analgesic: Witch hazel tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and itching caused by cold sores, due to the presence of volatile oils and other active constituents.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains high levels of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation caused by cold sores.

Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant contain active compounds such as gallic acid and tannins, which have antiviral and astringent properties that can help dry out cold sores and promote healing.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and inflammation caused by cold sores.
Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant are rich in volatile oils, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in early spring or late summer for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 50% vodka by volume in a clean glass jar.
Label the jar and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for maceration and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the reserved tincture to smaller glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place, ready for use within one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with cold sores because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce the swelling and pain associated with cold sores. The active compounds in St. John's wort, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. By applying St. John's wort tincture directly to the affected area, the herb's natural healing properties can help to speed up the healing process, reduce the duration of cold sores, and alleviate symptoms such as itching, burning, and tingling. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture can help to prevent future outbreaks by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: St. John's wort tincture has antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores by inhibiting the herpes simplex virus, the causative agent of cold sores, thanks to the active constituents hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of St. John's wort tincture can help alleviate pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores, due to the active constituent hyperforin.

Antioxidant properties: St. John's wort tincture contains antioxidants that can help protect skin cells from damage and promote healing, which is important for cold sores as they can cause skin damage and irritation, thanks to the active constituents hypericin and hyperforin.

Wound-healing properties: St. John's wort tincture has been shown to promote wound healing, which can help speed up the recovery process for cold sores, due to the active constituents hypericin and hyperforin.
Analgesic properties: St. John's wort tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce discomfort and pain associated with cold sores, thanks to the active constituent hyperforin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: St. John's wort flowers are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating cold sores.

Leaves: The leaves of St. John's wort contain high levels of hypericin and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antiviral and wound-healing properties, making them useful for cold sore treatment.
Buds: The buds of St. John's wort are a potent source of active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, aiding in the healing of cold sores.

Stems: The stems of St. John's wort contain valuable bioactive compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been found to have antiviral and wound-healing properties, beneficial for treating cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves for tincture preparation.
Combine one part of the plant material with four parts of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids after pressing.
Store the tincture in amber glass bottles at room temperature, using within one year of preparation or longer if refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with cold sores because it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of the virus that causes cold sores, thereby helping to speed up the healing process. Additionally, lavender tincture can help to soothe and moisturize the affected area, providing relief from the discomfort and itchiness that often accompanies cold sores. By using lavender tincture at the first sign of a cold sore, you can help to minimize its severity and duration, and get back to feeling your best.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: English lavender tincture can help reduce the severity of cold sores by inhibiting the herpes simplex virus, thanks to the presence of compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate.

Anti-inflammatory property: Its anti-inflammatory effects help alleviate pain, swelling, and redness associated with cold sores, due to the action of active constituents like camphor and caryophyllene.
Wound-healing property: Lavender tincture promotes faster healing of cold sores by stimulating tissue regeneration, attributable to its rich content of eucalyptol and α-terpineol.
Analgesic property: The tincture can help relieve pain caused by cold sores, as it contains compounds like linalyl acetate and lavandulyl acetate that have natural pain-relieving properties.

Immunomodulatory property: English lavender tincture can help strengthen the immune system, enabling the body to better fight off the herpes simplex virus, due to the presence of active constituents like 4-terpineol and geraniol.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing cold sore inflammation and preventing the virus from spreading.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, aiding in healing cold sores and preventing infection.
Buds: High in essential oils, which have antiviral and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain and promote faster healing of cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom, selecting buds and stems for optimal oil content.
Chop fresh or dried flowers into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction, using scissors or herb chopper.
Combine 250g of chopped flowers with 500ml of organic vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring flower material is completely covered by the solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate maceration and extraction of essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the resulting tincture for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with cold sore because it contains antiviral properties that can prevent the herpes simplex virus from replicating, which is the main cause of cold sores. Its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects can also help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. Additionally, lemon balm tincture has been shown to possess antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By applying lemon balm tincture to cold sores, it can help speed up the healing process, reduce the severity of symptoms, and prevent future outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Lemon balm tincture contains active constituents like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids that can inhibit the herpes simplex virus, the cause of cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemon balm tincture, due to its rosmarinic acid content, can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.
Immune-boosting property: Lemon balm tincture can help strengthen the immune system, aiding the body in fighting off viral infections like cold sores.
Antioxidant property: The high antioxidant content of lemon balm tincture, mainly from its rosmarinic acid and eugenol, can help protect skin cells from damage and support healing.

Nervine property: Lemon balm tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress-induced cold sore outbreaks. This property is attributed to its acid citral and citronellal content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for cold sores due to their antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them useful for treating cold sores.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain therapeutic compounds that can help soothe and heal cold sores.

Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm contain essential oils and other compounds that have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a useful addition to medicinal tinctures for cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves and stems in quantities of approximately 20% of the total tincture volume.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and mix with 200 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:3 herb-to-solvent by weight.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar or container, secure the lid tightly, and store in a cool dark place for six weeks.
After six weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and label it with the date of preparation, ingredients, and recommended dosage of 20-30 drops up to three times daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cold sores with herbal tinctures is by using lemon balm and St. John's Wort. Lemon balm has antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores, while St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory and wound-healing effects that can help soothe the pain and promote healing. Mix a few drops of each tincture in a glass of water and drink it three times a day. Additionally, you can apply the tinctures directly to the cold sore using a cotton swab several times a day for faster relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cold sores that are treated with herbal tinctures include canker sores and fever blisters. These conditions can cause discomfort and pain, similar to cold sores. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from echinacea, licorice root, and lemon balm, have been used traditionally to treat these ailments. Echinacea is believed to boost the immune system, while licorice root and lemon balm have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal tinctures, as they can interact with other medications and may have side effects.
